Title: Insights into the missing apiosylation step in flavonoid apiosides biosynthesis of Leguminosae plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regio-selectively and efficiently catalyzes 4′/4-O-glycosylation of liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin to form liquiritin and isoliquiritin, improving production of isoliquiritin apioside in tobacco engineering
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Liquiritin apioside shows potent antitussive activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and rhizomes of Glycyrrhiza uralensis; engineered Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Co-expression similar to GuCHS, GuCHR, and GuApiGT in G. uralensis; selected from root-enriched coexpression context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient pathway engineering in Nicotiana benthamiana; enzyme assay; LC/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GuGT53 was discovered from G. uralensis as a regio-selective 4′/4-O-glycosyltransferase for liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin. 2. Replacing GuGT14 with GuGT53 reduced byproduct formation and improved isoliquiritin apioside production in tobacco. 3. GuGT53 supports efficient precursor formation for downstream apiosylation by GuApiGT.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as an upstream pathway-supporting glycosyltransferase in the engineered de novo apioside pathway rather than as the terminal apiosyltransferase.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase; UGT79B74; GT-B fold; PSPG box with unique 45-aa motif containing RLGSDH
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose from UDP-apiose to the 2″-OH of sugar moieties on flavonoid glycosides, producing 2″-O-apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavonoid glycosides such as liquiritin and isoliquiritin; UDP-apiose as sugar donor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Liquiritin apioside; Isoliquiritin apioside; other flavonoid 2″-O-apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Missing terminal apiosylation step identified; key terminal determinant for phenolic apioside formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently and regio-selectively produces flavonoid 2″-O-apiosides and enables de novo biosynthesis of at least 10 flavonoid apiosides in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Liquiritin apioside has potent antitussive activity; flavonoid apiosides are bioactive natural products and may play hormone-like roles in plant growth regulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and rhizomes of Glycyrrhiza uralensis; engineered Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in roots relative to cortex and leaves; co-expressed with GuCHS and GuCHR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; RT-PCR cloning; recombinant expression and enzyme assay; LC/MS; kinetic analysis; crystal structure; site-directed mutagenesis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GuApiGT was identified as the missing phenolic apiosyltransferase from G. uralensis and almost completely converted liquiritin and isoliquiritin into their 2″-O-apiosides. 2. The enzyme shows strict sugar donor selectivity for UDP-apiose and contains a unique 45-aa PSPG box with the RLGSDH motif. 3. GuApiGT enabled de novo biosynthesis of multiple flavonoid apiosides in tobacco when combined with upstream pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA945816; structural data PDB 8HZZ
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Optimal activity at pH 8.0 and 37°C; divalent cations can suppress activity; transient Agrobacterium expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strictly prefers UDP-apiose over UDP-xylose and UDP-glucose; accepts 37 glycoside substrates but not free flavonoids; key residues/motif affecting sugar donor selectivity were defined structurally.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of bioactive flavonoid apiosides, especially liquiritin apioside and related compounds, in plant chassis systems; can be combined with upstream donor-supply and flavonoid modules for de novo production.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with Leguminosae-type 45-aa PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confirmed as an apiosyltransferase from Pueraria thomsonii
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtApiGT was identified among Leguminosae candidate genes filtered by the unique PSPG box. 2. Recombinant enzyme assays confirmed its apiosyltransferase activity. 3. Supports generality of the Leguminosae apiosyltransferase motif.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate mining from transcriptome databases; accession OQ230795
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four additional experimentally validated apiosyltransferases discovered in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with Leguminosae-type 45-aa PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confirmed as an apiosyltransferase from Spatholobus suberectus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis/cloning and enzyme catalysis assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsApiGT was found by motif-guided mining of Leguminosae sequences. 2. Functional assay validated its apiosyltransferase activity. 3. Reinforces conservation of the apiosyltransferase function in Leguminosae.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate mining from transcriptome databases; accession OQ230794

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with Leguminosae-type 45-aa PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confirmed as an apiosyltransferase from Glycyrrhiza glabra
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GgApiGT was experimentally identified as an apiosyltransferase. 2. Its activity supports that closely related Glycyrrhiza species share conserved flavonoid apiosylation enzymes. 3. It belongs to the Leguminosae-specific ApiGT clade defined by the RLGSDH-associated PSPG signature.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate mining from transcriptome databases; accession OQ230797
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly conserved with other characterized Leguminosae ApiGTs.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with Leguminosae-type 45-aa PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confirmed as an apiosyltransferase from Glycyrrhiza inflata
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GiApiGT was validated as a functional apiosyltransferase. 2. It extends the ApiGT activity across another Glycyrrhiza species. 3. Its discovery supports motif-based mining of Leguminosae apiosyltransferases.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate mining from transcriptome databases; accession OQ230796
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly similar to the other characterized ApiGTs.
